title: lysimachia remyi subsp. kipahuluensis
intro: Lysimachia remyi subsp. kipahuluensis, also known as Kipahulu lygodium or Kipahulu lemongrass, is a rare and endemic plant species found in Hawaii. It is a type of flowering plant in the primrose family, characterized by its fragrant, lemon-scented leaves and stems. The plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as colds, fever, and skin conditions. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting digestion.
